title: Non-Wheat Flours Guide
content:
NON-WHEAT FLOURS

Corn Flour/Meal: Stone-ground retains germ for superior flavor. Yellow has more vitamin A. Use in corn breads; mix with other flours for baking.

Rice Flour: Makes close, delicate textured cakes. Substitute 1 cup minus 2 tablespoons for 1 cup all-purpose. Waxy types (mochika) for sauces only.

Rye Flour: Combined with wheat flour in most recipes as it lacks gluten elasticity. Substitution: 1⅓ cups rye for 1 cup all-purpose.

Soy Flour: High protein and fat. Stir before measuring. Substitute 2 tablespoons soy plus ⅞ cup all-purpose for 1 cup all-purpose.

Buckwheat Flour: High biological value. Use ¼ cup buckwheat to ¾ cup other flour.

Oat Flour/Oatmeal: Combine with wheat up to one-third. Soak in boiling water with shortening before adding leaven.

Triticale Flour: Wheat-rye hybrid; low gluten. Mix with higher-gluten flours for bread.
